{"problem_standardized_math":"Implement Algorithm 14.3 in Matlab, using \\(\\eta = 0.99\\) in (14.38). Test your code on a linear programming problem (14.1) constructed by choosing \\(A\\) randomly and then defining \\(x\\), \\(s\\), \\(\\lambda\\), \\(c\\), and \\(b\\) by\n\\[\nx_i=\n\\begin{cases}\n\\text{a random positive number}, & i=1,2,\\ldots,m,\\\\\n0, & i=m+1,m+2,\\ldots,n,\n\\end{cases}\n\\]\n\\[\ns_i=\n\\begin{cases}\n\\text{a random positive number}, & i=m+1,m+2,\\ldots,n,\\\\\n0, & i=1,2,\\ldots,m,\n\\end{cases}\n\\]\n\\[\n\\lambda = \\text{a random vector},\n\\]\n\\[\nc = A^T\\lambda + s,\n\\]\n\\[\nb = Ax.\n\\]\nChoose the starting point \\((x^0,\\lambda^0,s^0)\\) so that the components of \\(x^0\\) and \\(s^0\\) are large positive values."}
